ORDER GRANTING DEFENDANT CALAVERAS' MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE ITS EXPERT "PARED-DOWN" LIST PURSUANT TO CCP 2034.720

ASBESTOS DISCOVERY 220, DEFENDANT CALAVERAS ASBESTOS, LTD.'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE UNTIMELY EXPERT 'PARED-DOWN' LIST PURSUANT TO C.C.P. 2034.720 IS GRANTED. GOOD CAUSE EXISTS FOR THE RELIEF SOUGHT. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST THAT CALAVERAS BEAR THE COST OF DEPOSING MR. SPIELMAN IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF WOULD TYPICALLY INCUR THE COST OF DEPOSING THIS WITNESS AND CALAVERAS' FAILURE TO TIMELY SUBMIT ITS 'PARED DOWN' LIST WAS THE RESULT OF MISTAKE, INADVERTENCE, SURPRISE OR EXCUSABLE NEGLECT WITH NO PREJUDICE TO PLAINTIFF. CALAVERAS' REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF'S 'OPPOSITION' IN THE FORM OF SEEKING AN ORDER THAT CALAVERAS BEAR THE COST OF DEPOSING MR. SPIELMAN, WHILE UNSUCCESSFUL, DID NOT LACK 'SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, MATTER NOT REPORTED.

Off Calendar Jun-02-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Elizabeth R. Bain, Esq. of FOLEY & MANSFIELD (510-590-9500), appearing for defendant / moving party Calaveras Asbestos, Ltd.
Kimberly Chu, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Welton Oliver

RULING:
DEFENDANT CALAVERAS ASBESTOS, LTD.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Argued and the court took the matter under submission. Both sides submitted proposed orders for the Court consideration. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)

Notice Of Motion For Leave To File Its Expert "Pared-Down" List Pursuant To C.C.P. § 2034.720
Off Calendar Jan-28-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Elizabeth R. Bain, Esq. of FOLEY & MANSFIELD (510-590-9500), appearing for defendant / moving party CALAVERAS ASBESTOS, LTD.
James P. Nevin, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Welton Oliver, Jr.

RULING:
Defendant CALAVERAS ASBESTOS, LTD.'s motion for leave to file untimely expert 'pared-down' list Pursuant To C.C.P. 2034.720. No argument was made and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant's motion is granted. Good cause exists for the relief sought. Plaintiff's request that Calaveras bear the cost of deposing Mr. Spielman is denied. Plaintiff would typically incur the cost of deposing this witness and Calaveras' failure to timely submit its 'pared down' list was the result of mistake, inadvertence, surprise or excusable neglect with no prejudice to plaintiff. Calaveras' request for monetary sanctions is denied. Plaintiff's 'opposition' in the form of seeking an order that Calaveras bear the cost of deposing Mr. Spielman, while unsuccessful, did not lack 'substantial justification. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Matter not reported.
